meta.name: OASIS Overall Anxiety Severity and Impairment Scale
meta.fullName: Overall Anxiety Severity and Impairment Scale
meta.description: The OASIS is a brief 5-item scale that assesses the frequency, intensity, and functional impairment of overall anxiety, applicable across all anxiety disorder types.
meta.author: Norman et al. (2006)
meta.reference: Norman SB, et al. Development and validation of an Overall Anxiety Severity and Impairment Scale (OASIS). Depression and Anxiety. 2006;23(4):245-249.
meta.instruction: Please answer about your anxiety over the past week.

questions.text: In the past week, how often have you felt anxious
When you have felt anxious, how intense or severe was your anxiety
How often did you avoid situations, places, or activities because of anxiety
How much did anxiety interfere with your ability to do work, school, or household tasks
How much did anxiety interfere with your social life and relationships

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your overall anxiety severity is within the normal range, with no significant anxiety-related distress or functional impairment detected. Your current emotional state appears stable and well-regulated.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current mental health habits and coping strategies
Keep up regular exercise and relaxation routines that support emotional well-being
Nurture your social support network as a protective factor against future stress

ranges.label: Mild Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score indicates mild overall anxiety. You may occasionally feel tense, worried, or on edge, but the impact on your work, social life, and daily activities remains limited.
suggestions: Learn and regularly practice deep breathing and progressive muscle relaxation techniques
Maintain a consistent routine of physical exercise to help regulate your anxiety levels
Identify your specific anxiety triggers and develop proactive strategies to manage them
Consider consulting a mental health professional if your anxiety continues to increase over time

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests moderate anxiety that may be noticeably interfering with your daily functioning. Work performance, social interactions, and routine activities may be affected by persistent worry.
suggestions: Consider seeking help from a counselor, psychologist, or psychiatrist for professional guidance
Learn structured anxiety management techniques such as cognitive-behavioral strategies and thought challenging
Reduce or eliminate excessive caffeine and alcohol consumption as these can worsen anxiety symptoms
Establish consistent daily routines with dedicated time for relaxation and enjoyable activities
Practice exposure techniques by gradually and safely confronting avoided situations to build confidence

ranges.label: Severe Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe overall anxiety that is likely significantly impairing your occupational, social, and personal functioning. Persistent and intense anxiety at this level warrants prompt professional attention.
suggestions: Seek professional psychological or psychiatric treatment as soon as possible for comprehensive evaluation
Inform trusted family members or friends about your condition so they can provide meaningful support
Work to gradually face anxiety-provoking situations with professional guidance rather than relying on avoidance
If you are in crisis or experiencing overwhelming distress, contact a mental health crisis hotline immediately
